Course Title: AI in Safety-Critical Environments: What Can Go Wrong?
Category: Agriculture & Resources: Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing, Mining, Energy
Who is this for: Farm managers, mining supervisors, energy plant operators, safety officers, field technicians, agricultural workers, oil rig personnel, mine workers, equipment operators, site supervisors
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Safety, Agriculture, Mining, Energy, Field Operations, Health and Safety, Risk Management
Description: How AI errors can impact physical safety in farms, rigs, and mines.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with everyday technology and a general awareness of how farms, energy sites, or mining operations function in practice are all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify common AI failure points in safety-critical agricultural, mining, and energy environments, and explain how these errors can lead to real-world physical risks for workers and operations. You will also apply a practical framework for recognising warning signs of AI-related hazards on farms, oil rigs, and in mines, helping you contribute to safer decision-making in your workplace.
